name: Crystal Falls
overview: Crystal Falls is a master-planned community in Leander TX built around a 3,500-acre golf course and nature preserve. Developed starting in the early 2000s, it is one of the most established and sought-after neighborhoods in the entire Austin metro north corridor. Large lots, mature live oaks, winding creeks, and sweeping Hill Country terrain define the landscape. Multiple phases of development mean homes range from early 2000s traditional styles to more contemporary builds completed after 2015. The community is anchored by the Crystal Falls Golf Club, a semi-private 18-hole course that gives the neighborhood its identity and provides residents with scenic fairway views. Neighborhood pools, playgrounds, and a strong HOA community events calendar round out a lifestyle that consistently attracts families relocating from Austin proper.
lifestyle: Family-friendly with golf course views, mature trees, and one of Leander's strongest community identities. Ideal for families wanting space and nature without sacrificing access to major employers.

faqs.q: What are Crystal Falls property taxes?
faqs.a: Crystal Falls sits in the City of Leander with a combined rate around 2.28% in 2026, one of the lower rates in the area. Original sections built before 2005 predate most MUD districts, keeping the overall burden relatively modest compared to newer Leander communities.

faqs.q: What schools serve Crystal Falls?
faqs.a: Crystal Falls is zoned to Leander ISD — Whitestone Elementary, Leander Middle School, and Leander High School. All three are well-regarded within the district with strong extracurricular programs.

faqs.q: Is Crystal Falls a good neighborhood?
faqs.a: Crystal Falls consistently ranks among Leander's most desirable neighborhoods due to the golf course, large lots, mature trees, and top-rated schools. Resale values have held exceptionally well through multiple market cycles.

faqs.q: How far is Crystal Falls from Austin?
faqs.a: Crystal Falls is approximately 35–45 minutes from Downtown Austin via the 183A toll road depending on traffic. The commute to the Domain or Apple's campus is closer to 25–30 minutes.

faqs.q: What is the HOA fee in Crystal Falls?
faqs.a: Crystal Falls HOA fees are approximately $60/month, covering common area maintenance, pool operations, park upkeep, and the community events program.

name: Bryson
overview: Bryson is one of Leander's newest and most amenity-rich master-planned communities, developed in the mid-2010s in the northwestern corner of the city. Built by KB Home and Centex (now part of Pulte), Bryson is known for its resort-style amenities including a lazy river, resort pool, splash pad, and a fully staffed community center. The neighborhood was designed with a lifestyle-first philosophy — wide sidewalks, pocket parks, a catch-and-release pond, and extensive trails connect every section. Homes are primarily single-family detached ranging from entry-level offerings to larger move-up product. Because Bryson was built on land requiring new utility infrastructure, it carries a Williamson County MUD district which adds approximately 0.35% to the base tax rate. Buyers who prioritize amenities and new construction quality will find Bryson compelling; those sensitive to total cost of ownership should calculate the full tax burden carefully.
lifestyle: Resort-living feel with a strong community social calendar. Popular with young families and first-time buyers who want maximum amenities per dollar spent. Active neighborhood Facebook group and HOA event programming.

faqs.q: Does Bryson have a MUD district?
faqs.a: Yes. Bryson is served by Williamson County MUD #22, which adds approximately 0.35% to the base Leander tax rate, bringing the total to around 2.47% in 2026. MUD bonds are projected to mature in the late 2030s, after which the rate will decline.

faqs.q: What schools serve Bryson?
faqs.a: Bryson is zoned to Leander ISD — Jim Plain Elementary, Danielson Middle School, and Glenn High School. Glenn is a newer campus that opened in 2016 to serve Leander's growing northwest corridor.

faqs.q: What amenities does Bryson have?
faqs.a: Bryson features a lazy river, resort pool, splash pad, fitness center, catch-and-release pond, dog park, and extensive trail network — some of the best neighborhood amenities in Williamson County.

faqs.q: How far is Bryson from Austin?
faqs.a: Bryson is located in northwest Leander, making the commute to Downtown Austin approximately 40–50 minutes via 183A. The Domain is closer, around 30–35 minutes.

faqs.q: Is Bryson still building?
faqs.a: As of 2026, Bryson continues to add new sections. Buyers can still purchase new construction directly from builders like KB Home and Pulte, with delivery timelines varying by phase.

name: Travisso
overview: Travisso is Cedar Park's premier luxury master-planned community, perched atop the Balcones Escarpment with sweeping Hill Country views that stretch for miles. Developed by Toll Brothers and Taylor Morrison beginning in the early 2010s, Travisso was designed to capture the elevated terrain of western Cedar Park — dramatic ridgelines, live oak-studded canyons, and sunset vistas visible from countless homesites. The community's clubhouse is a showpiece: resort-style pool, tennis courts, event pavilion, and fitness center are built to match the premium pricing of the homes themselves. Travisso commands Cedar Park's highest price points, with custom and semi-custom product starting above $500,000 and luxury estates reaching $1.2M+. Despite the prestige, the community's MUD tax rate is relatively moderate compared to newer Leander projects, and its location provides excellent access to both the 183A corridor and Lake Travis recreation.
amenities: Scenic overlooks and Hill Country view corridors
lifestyle: Upscale Hill Country living with panoramic views and resort amenities. Attracts executives, physicians, and tech professionals seeking prestige and privacy within commuting range of Austin's major employment centers.

faqs.q: Is Travisso in Cedar Park or Leander?
faqs.a: Travisso's mailing address is Cedar Park, TX, and it falls within Cedar Park city limits and Leander ISD. The community is in Williamson County.

faqs.q: What is the tax rate in Travisso?
faqs.a: Travisso's combined property tax rate is approximately 2.35% in 2026, including a modest MUD district for infrastructure financing. On a $650,000 home after homestead exemption, expect roughly $12,000–$13,000/year.

faqs.q: What schools serve Travisso?
faqs.a: Travisso is zoned to Leander ISD — CC Mason Elementary, Running Brushy Middle School, and Leander High School. All are consistently rated among the top schools in the district.

faqs.q: How far is Travisso from downtown Austin?
faqs.a: Travisso is approximately 35–50 minutes from downtown Austin depending on traffic and route. The 183A toll road provides the fastest connection.

faqs.q: What makes Travisso worth the premium price?
faqs.a: Travisso offers Hill Country views from nearly every homesite, top-tier builders, luxury amenities, and Leander ISD schools — a combination that has supported strong appreciation and low days-on-market for well-priced homes.

name: Larkspur
overview: Larkspur is a boutique master-planned community in central Leander developed primarily by David Weekley Homes. Unlike the sprawling mega-developments that define much of Leander's growth, Larkspur was intentionally designed at a more intimate scale — curated streetscapes, alley-loaded garages, front porches, and tree-lined streets create a neighborhood feel that larger communities struggle to replicate. The community sits just east of 183A with direct access to Leander's growing retail and restaurant corridor along Crystal Falls Parkway. David Weekley's reputation for energy efficiency, design flexibility, and customer service has attracted discerning buyers who want a well-built home without sacrificing neighborhood character. HOA amenities include a community pool, playgrounds, and maintained green spaces. Larkspur is particularly popular among second-time buyers upgrading from older Leander or Cedar Park homes.
lifestyle: Boutique neighborhood feel with high-quality David Weekley construction. Attracts move-up buyers and remote workers who value design, energy efficiency, and a true neighborhood atmosphere over mega-development scale.

faqs.q: Who builds in Larkspur Leander?
faqs.a: Larkspur is primarily a David Weekley Homes community. David Weekley is known for design flexibility, energy-efficient construction, and strong customer satisfaction ratings.

faqs.q: What is the tax rate in Larkspur?
faqs.a: Larkspur's combined tax rate is approximately 2.44% in 2026, including a MUD district for infrastructure. This is slightly above mid-range for Leander but reflects the newer construction vintage.

faqs.q: What schools serve Larkspur?
faqs.a: Larkspur is zoned to Leander ISD — Pleasant Hill Elementary, Leander Middle School, and Leander High School.

faqs.q: Is Larkspur walkable?
faqs.a: Larkspur's alley-loaded design creates more walkable, front-porch-oriented streetscapes than most Leander communities. While still car-dependent for most errands, the neighborhood's internal connectivity is excellent.

faqs.q: How does Larkspur compare to Crystal Falls?
faqs.a: Crystal Falls offers larger lots, golf course access, and mature trees but with an older housing stock. Larkspur offers newer builds, better energy efficiency, and a more intentionally designed streetscape at a similar price point.

name: Northline
overview: Northline is Leander's most ambitious urban planning experiment — a mixed-use, walkable village concept built adjacent to the Capital MetroRail commuter rail station in central Leander. Developed by Taylor Morrison and the City of Leander as a transit-oriented development, Northline integrates residential, retail, restaurant, and office space in a dense, connected layout unlike anything else in Williamson County. Residents can walk to coffee shops, restaurants, and retail without a car — a rarity in suburban Texas. The MetroRail connection provides a traffic-free commute option to downtown Austin for those whose schedules align with the rail timetable. Homes range from townhomes and cottages to traditional single-family, all designed with smaller footprints and higher connectivity than conventional suburban product. Northline carries a MUD district rate due to its significant infrastructure investment.
lifestyle: Leander's most urban neighborhood by design. Ideal for commuters, remote workers who value walkability, and buyers seeking a community that functions more like a small town center than a conventional subdivision.

faqs.q: Can I take the train from Northline to Austin?
faqs.a: Yes. Northline is walkable to the Leander MetroRail station, the northern terminus of Capital Metro's Red Line commuter rail. The train runs weekdays to downtown Austin, the Domain, and intermediate stops.

faqs.q: What is the tax rate in Northline?
faqs.a: Northline's combined tax rate is approximately 2.52% in 2026, one of Leander's higher rates due to the MUD district financing the transit-oriented infrastructure.

faqs.q: What schools serve Northline?
faqs.a: Northline is zoned to Leander ISD — Jim Plain Elementary, Danielson Middle School, and Glenn High School.

faqs.q: Is Northline good for people who don't want to drive everywhere?
faqs.a: Northline is the best option in Williamson County for buyers prioritizing walkability. The mixed-use village concept means restaurants, coffee, and services are within walking distance, and the MetroRail offers a car-free Austin commute option.

faqs.q: Who are the builders in Northline?
faqs.a: Taylor Morrison is the primary builder in Northline's residential sections, with Milestone Community Builders active in select phases. Product types include cottages, townhomes, and detached single-family homes.

name: Deerbrooke
overview: Deerbrooke is one of Leander's newer master-planned communities, developed by Meritage Homes beginning around 2018 in the northeastern quadrant of the city. Meritage built its brand on energy-efficient construction, and Deerbrooke homes are among the best-insulated, most airtight new builds in the Austin market — a genuine long-term savings advantage in Texas's climate. The community features a resort-style pool, splash pad, open-air pavilion, and playgrounds, all maintained by an active HOA. Because Deerbrooke's infrastructure was financed through a MUD, the tax rate runs on the higher side for Leander — but buyers who plan to stay 10+ years will benefit as MUD bonds mature and rates decline. Proximity to Toll 45 and US-183 provides multiple routing options to major employment centers.
lifestyle: Energy-conscious families attracted to Meritage's industry-leading construction standards. Strong community bond due to newer, similarly-aged homeowner base. MUD rate is a known trade-off for fresh infrastructure and energy savings.

faqs.q: Are Deerbrooke homes energy efficient?
faqs.a: Yes. Meritage Homes builds to a proprietary energy efficiency standard with enhanced insulation, spray foam, low-E windows, and tight construction that typically results in 30–50% lower utility bills versus comparable older homes.

faqs.q: What is the tax rate in Deerbrooke?
faqs.a: Deerbrooke's combined tax rate is approximately 2.50% in 2026, including MUD district debt service. On a $400,000 home after homestead exemption, that's roughly $7,500/year.

faqs.q: What schools serve Deerbrooke?
faqs.a: Deerbrooke is zoned to Leander ISD — North Leander Elementary, Danielson Middle School, and Glenn High School.

faqs.q: Does Deerbrooke have a MUD?
faqs.a: Yes, Deerbrooke is served by Williamson County MUD #25 which adds approximately 0.28% to the base rate. As bond debt is retired over the next 15 years, this rate will decrease.

faqs.q: How new is Deerbrooke?
faqs.a: Deerbrooke began development around 2018 and continues to add phases. Most homes in the community are 2019–2025 vintage, meaning buyers get relatively new construction with manufacturer warranties still in effect.

name: Horizon Lake
overview: Horizon Lake is one of Leander's newer residential developments, positioned in the western reaches of the city near the corridor leading to Lake Travis. The community takes advantage of gently rolling terrain with Hill Country characteristics — cedar and live oak coverage, limestone outcroppings, and the visual variety that comes with natural grade changes. Horizon Lake's location appeals to buyers who want Leander's school district and tax advantages while living closer to Lake Travis recreation, Lago Vista amenities, and the scenic drive along FM 1431. Homes are primarily built by mid-range production builders with floor plans ranging from 1,800 to 3,500 square feet. The community is still in active development, with new phases continuing to add homes and planned amenity improvements.
lifestyle: Western Leander lifestyle with Hill Country character. Best suited for buyers who value outdoor recreation proximity — Lake Travis, parks, and scenic drives — over urban walkability.

faqs.q: How far is Horizon Lake from Lake Travis?
faqs.a: Horizon Lake is approximately 20 minutes from Lake Travis public access points and marinas along FM 1431, making it one of the closest Leander communities to the lake.

faqs.q: What is the tax rate in Horizon Lake?
faqs.a: Horizon Lake's combined tax rate is approximately 2.42% in 2026, including a MUD district. This is consistent with mid-range newer Leander developments.

faqs.q: What schools serve Horizon Lake?
faqs.a: Horizon Lake is zoned to Leander ISD — Bagdad Elementary, Leander Middle School, and Leander High School.

faqs.q: Is Horizon Lake still building?
faqs.a: Yes, Horizon Lake continues to add new sections as of 2026. New construction is available from multiple builders with typical delivery timelines of 6–12 months from contract.

faqs.q: What builders are in Horizon Lake?
faqs.a: Lennar, D.R. Horton, and CastleRock Communities are among the builders active in Horizon Lake, offering a range of floor plans and price points.

name: Block House Creek
overview: Block House Creek is one of Leander's most established neighborhoods, developed primarily in the 1990s along the namesake creek greenway. As an older community without MUD district financing, Block House Creek offers some of the lowest effective property tax rates in Leander — a significant advantage for cost-conscious buyers who don't require new construction. Homes are a mix of traditional ranch styles and two-story colonials on generous lots, many with mature trees that took decades to reach their current canopy coverage. The neighborhood's crown jewel is the Block House Creek Greenway, miles of hike-and-bike trails along the creek corridor connecting to Cedar Park and beyond. The community pool and recreation facilities are owned and operated by the HOA at remarkably low cost due to the paid-off infrastructure. Block House Creek is the go-to recommendation for buyers asking 'where can I get the most space and lowest taxes in Leander?'
amenities: Block House Creek Greenway trail system
lifestyle: Established, quiet neighborhood with the lowest HOA fees and tax rates in Leander. Popular with buyers who prioritize value, mature landscaping, and trail access over new construction.

faqs.q: Why are Block House Creek taxes so low?
faqs.a: Block House Creek was developed in the 1990s before MUD districts became standard in Williamson County. Without MUD debt service, residents pay only city, county, and school district taxes — among the lowest combined rates in Leander.

faqs.q: What schools serve Block House Creek?
faqs.a: Block House Creek is zoned to Leander ISD with students attending Block House Creek Elementary, Cedar Park Middle School, and Cedar Park High School.

faqs.q: Is Block House Creek a good neighborhood?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Block House Creek offers large lots with mature trees, an extensive greenway trail system, low taxes, and some of Leander's most affordable homes — making it consistently popular with value-focused buyers.

faqs.q: What is the HOA fee in Block House Creek?
faqs.a: Block House Creek HOA fees are approximately $25/month — one of the lowest in Leander — covering common area maintenance, the community pool, and trail upkeep.

faqs.q: Are homes in Block House Creek older?
faqs.a: Yes. Most Block House Creek homes were built in the 1990s through mid-2000s. Buyers should budget for potential updates but gain significant value through lower prices and tax rates.

name: Caballo Ranch
overview: Caballo Ranch is a newer master-planned community in northeastern Leander, developed on land that captures the rolling agricultural character of Williamson County before the city built up around it. The name nods to the horse ranch heritage of the area. Homes are primarily two-story single-family built by a mix of mid-range and move-up production builders, with floor plans ranging from 2,000 to 3,800 square feet. The community pool, splash pad, and playground are finished amenities that give Caballo Ranch a family-oriented identity. Portions of Caballo Ranch fall outside MUD boundaries, making certain sections more tax-efficient than comparable new Leander developments. Easy access to Toll 183A positions residents well for commutes to the Domain, downtown Austin, or Cedar Park's employment corridor. Active development continues in the community's later phases.
lifestyle: Family-focused community in northeast Leander with newer construction at more moderate prices than Leander's premium communities. Good option for buyers wanting space and no MUD overhead.

faqs.q: Is Caballo Ranch in a MUD district?
faqs.a: Most of Caballo Ranch falls outside MUD district boundaries, resulting in a combined tax rate of approximately 2.35% — meaningfully lower than many comparable new Leander communities with MUD districts.

faqs.q: What schools serve Caballo Ranch?
faqs.a: Caballo Ranch is zoned to Leander ISD — North Leander Elementary, Danielson Middle School, and Glenn High School.

faqs.q: What builders are in Caballo Ranch?
faqs.a: Lennar, Pacesetter Homes, and First Texas Homes are among the primary builders in Caballo Ranch, offering a range of floor plans for families at various price points.

faqs.q: How far is Caballo Ranch from the Domain?
faqs.a: Caballo Ranch residents can reach the Domain (Apple Campus, shopping, restaurants) in approximately 28–35 minutes via 183A — a manageable daily commute for most professionals.

faqs.q: Is Caballo Ranch still developing?
faqs.a: Yes. As of 2026, Caballo Ranch continues to add later phases with both new construction and resale inventory available. The community is expected to complete build-out within the next several years.

name: Bar W Ranch
overview: Bar W Ranch occupies Leander's western frontier — one of the last large land tracts within city limits to be developed, giving it a remoteness and openness that's increasingly rare in the Austin metro. The community takes its name from the ranching heritage of the land, and that spirit carries into the generous lot sizes, native landscaping requirements, and deliberate low-density design. Builders have emphasized outdoor living spaces, and many homesites back to greenbelts or creek setbacks. FM 2769 provides the primary routing in and out, connecting to 183A and RR 620 for broader access. Bar W Ranch is a newer community still in active development, with infrastructure improvements and commercial development along its primary corridors still maturing. For buyers who want space, quiet, and a western Hill Country character within Leander ISD, Bar W Ranch is the best current option.
lifestyle: Western Leander's quietest, most spacious community. Attracts buyers who are willing to trade commute time for larger lots, less density, and a rural character not found in central Leander developments.

faqs.q: Is Bar W Ranch far from Austin?
faqs.a: Bar W Ranch is on Leander's western edge, making it one of the longer commutes in the area — approximately 45–60 minutes to downtown Austin. Buyers should weigh this against the space and quiet the community offers.

faqs.q: What is the tax rate in Bar W Ranch?
faqs.a: Bar W Ranch's combined tax rate is approximately 2.38% in 2026, including a MUD district. This is moderate by Leander standards for newer construction.

faqs.q: What schools serve Bar W Ranch?
faqs.a: Bar W Ranch is zoned to Leander ISD — Bagdad Elementary, Leander Middle School, and Leander High School.

faqs.q: What makes Bar W Ranch unique?
faqs.a: Bar W Ranch offers the largest lot sizes and lowest density of any active Leander development, with many homesites backing to greenbelt or creek features. It's the best choice in Leander for buyers who prioritize space over commute time.

faqs.q: Who builds in Bar W Ranch?
faqs.a: Scott Felder Homes, Milestone Community Builders, and Coventry Homes are among the active builders in Bar W Ranch, known for customizable floor plans and outdoor living emphasis.

name: Twin Creeks
overview: Twin Creeks is Cedar Park's signature master-planned community, anchored by the Twin Creeks Country Club and its 18-hole golf course. Developed in the mid-2000s, Twin Creeks attracted the wave of tech professionals and medical workers arriving with companies like Dell and Scott & White (now Baylor Scott & White) that built their campuses in the Cedar Park / Round Rock corridor. The community is a full-service lifestyle destination: golf, swim, tennis, and fitness are all available within the gates of the country club. Multiple product types span from traditional courtyard homes to executive estates, with significant diversity in lot size and home style throughout the community's multiple sections. Mature landscaping, creek corridors, and the rolling terrain of west Cedar Park give Twin Creeks a settled, established feel. The community draws professionals from the major employers along US-183 and US-35 who value quality of life over proximity to Austin proper.
lifestyle: Established executive community with country club lifestyle. Popular with Dell, Apple, and healthcare professionals. One of Cedar Park's most prestigious addresses with strong appreciation history.

faqs.q: Is Twin Creeks a gated community?
faqs.a: Twin Creeks has a guarded entry but is not fully gated. The country club facilities require membership, but the residential streets are accessible.

faqs.q: What is the tax rate in Twin Creeks?
faqs.a: Twin Creeks enjoys one of Cedar Park's more favorable tax rates at approximately 2.22% in 2026. The community predates most MUD districts in the area, keeping rates lower than newer developments.

faqs.q: What schools serve Twin Creeks?
faqs.a: Twin Creeks is zoned to Leander ISD — CC Mason Elementary, Running Brushy Middle School, and Leander High School — all consistently high-performing campuses.

faqs.q: How far is Twin Creeks from the Domain?
faqs.a: Twin Creeks is approximately 20–28 minutes from the Domain and Apple's campus via 183A — one of the most convenient commutes in the Cedar Park market for tech workers.

faqs.q: Is Twin Creeks still building?
faqs.a: Twin Creeks is a built-out community with primarily resale inventory available. Occasional estate lots and teardown opportunities exist, but new construction is largely complete.

name: Buttercup Creek
overview: Buttercup Creek is one of Cedar Park's most beloved established neighborhoods, developed throughout the 1990s and early 2000s in central Cedar Park. The community name comes from the wildflowers that once blanketed the meadows before development — an echo of the area's natural heritage that continues in the extensive park system woven through the neighborhood. Buttercup Creek's greatest asset is its school district performance: Leander ISD's consistently top-rated elementary and middle schools serve the community, making it the go-to recommendation for families prioritizing education above all else. The neighborhood's mature tree canopy, wide streets, and absence of MUD taxes create a combination of livability and affordability that's rare in the current market. Multiple parks, a community pool, tennis courts, and trail connections keep the neighborhood active and social. For value-focused buyers who want great schools, low taxes, and community feel, Buttercup Creek is hard to beat.
lifestyle: Cedar Park's most family-oriented established neighborhood. Attracting a mix of longtime residents and families relocating for Leander ISD schools. The best combination of school quality and tax efficiency in Cedar Park.

faqs.q: Why is Buttercup Creek so popular with families?
faqs.a: Buttercup Creek offers Leander ISD's best schools, no MUD taxes, a mature neighborhood feel, and some of Cedar Park's lowest HOA fees — a combination that consistently makes it one of the most in-demand resale markets in Williamson County.

faqs.q: What is the tax rate in Buttercup Creek?
faqs.a: Buttercup Creek has one of Cedar Park's lowest combined tax rates at approximately 2.14% in 2026, as it predates MUD districts and benefits from established infrastructure.

faqs.q: What schools serve Buttercup Creek?
faqs.a: Buttercup Creek is zoned to Leander ISD — Westside Elementary, Cedar Park Middle School, and Cedar Park High School, all of which consistently earn top ratings from TEA.

faqs.q: Are homes in Buttercup Creek older?
faqs.a: Most Buttercup Creek homes were built between 1992 and 2008. Buyers should expect homes that may need cosmetic updates but gain significant value from mature lots and low-cost ownership.

faqs.q: How close is Buttercup Creek to shopping?
faqs.a: Buttercup Creek is centrally located in Cedar Park with easy access to Lakeline Mall, 1890 Ranch, and the HEB Plus corridor — some of Williamson County's best retail.

name: Brushy Creek
overview: Brushy Creek is one of Central Texas's most unusual communities — a master-planned development governed by its own Municipal Utility District (Brushy Creek MUD) that functions as a quasi-municipal government providing water, wastewater, parks, and recreation services directly to residents. The result is one of the most impressive park systems in all of Williamson County: over 3,000 acres of parks, trails, sports complexes, swimming pools, and recreation centers operated at a scale that no neighborhood HOA could match. Brushy Creek MUD manages facilities including multiple competition-grade pools, extensive sports fields, pavilions, and the crown jewel Brushy Creek Trail, a 30+ mile multi-use trail that connects through Cedar Park and Round Rock. Homes throughout Brushy Creek span multiple decades of development from the 1980s through 2010s, with price points ranging from starter homes to executive product depending on the specific section.
commuteAustin: 30–40 min via US-183 or Parmer Lane
builders: Various — Brushy Creek is a large multi-builder community
lifestyle: Unmatched recreation infrastructure in any Austin-area neighborhood. Ideal for athletic families, trail runners, cyclists, and anyone who places a premium on outdoor public amenities. The MUD model means no HOA politics, just professionally managed facilities.

faqs.q: What is Brushy Creek MUD?
faqs.a: Brushy Creek MUD (Municipal Utility District) is a special-purpose government entity that provides water, wastewater, drainage, parks, and recreation services to the Brushy Creek community. It functions like a mini-city for its residents.

faqs.q: Does Brushy Creek have an HOA?
faqs.a: Most of Brushy Creek does not have a traditional HOA. The MUD serves the neighborhood-wide functions that HOAs perform elsewhere, funded through the MUD tax rate rather than monthly HOA dues.

faqs.q: What are the parks like in Brushy Creek?
faqs.a: Brushy Creek's MUD-operated park system covers over 3,000 acres with multiple pools, 30+ miles of trails, sports fields, pavilions, and recreation centers — among the best community recreational infrastructure in the entire Austin metro.

faqs.q: What schools serve Brushy Creek?
faqs.a: Brushy Creek is zoned to Leander ISD — Cypress Elementary, Cedar Park Middle School, and Cedar Park High School.

faqs.q: Is Brushy Creek a good investment?
faqs.a: Brushy Creek homes have historically held value well due to the exceptional MUD-operated amenities, Leander ISD schools, and Cedar Park location. The park system provides a quality-of-life advantage difficult to find elsewhere at this price point.

name: Forest Oaks
overview: Forest Oaks is a well-established Cedar Park neighborhood developed primarily in the late 1990s and early 2000s in central Cedar Park near the US-183 and FM 1431 intersection. The neighborhood's name reflects its defining feature — a genuine tree canopy of mature live oaks that shade streets and yards in a way that takes decades to develop and cannot be replicated in new construction. Homes are single-family detached on traditional lots, built in a mix of brick and stone ranch and two-story styles that characterized Central Texas suburban development of the era. Forest Oaks benefits from Cedar Park's lower base tax rate relative to Leander, no MUD district, and proximity to the city's retail core along US-183. The schools assigned to Forest Oaks are consistently among the highest-rated in Leander ISD. A quiet, family-oriented atmosphere and low HOA overhead make Forest Oaks one of Cedar Park's most practical long-term value plays.
commuteAustin: 32–42 min via US-183 or Parmer Lane
lifestyle: Quiet, established neighborhood with unbeatable mature-tree character and low overhead. Ideal for families, retirees, or buyers seeking value and stability over new construction.

faqs.q: What is the tax rate in Forest Oaks?
faqs.a: Forest Oaks has a combined tax rate of approximately 2.16% in 2026 — one of Cedar Park's lowest, as the neighborhood predates MUD districts and carries no special district taxes.

faqs.q: What schools serve Forest Oaks?
faqs.a: Forest Oaks is zoned to Leander ISD — Forest North Elementary, Running Brushy Middle School, and Cedar Park High School. All three are consistently rated in the top tier of Williamson County schools.

faqs.q: Is Forest Oaks a good neighborhood?
faqs.a: Forest Oaks is one of Cedar Park's most consistently desirable resale neighborhoods due to its mature trees, location, school zoning, and low tax overhead. Homes sell quickly and appreciate steadily.

faqs.q: Are there new homes available in Forest Oaks?
faqs.a: Forest Oaks is a built-out community with no new construction. All available inventory is resale.

faqs.q: How close is Forest Oaks to shopping?
faqs.a: Forest Oaks is among Cedar Park's most centrally located neighborhoods, with immediate access to 1890 Ranch retail, US-183 restaurants, and HEB — making it highly convenient for everyday errands.

name: Walsh Ranch / Quarry
overview: Walsh Ranch and the adjacent Quarry section represent Cedar Park's newest large-scale residential development, situated on land that once served limestone quarrying operations in western Cedar Park. The quarry heritage gives the community its distinctive visual character — retained limestone terracing, boulder landscaping, and a nod to the industrial past in the community's branding. Development began in the early 2020s and is actively continuing, meaning buyers have access to new construction across multiple price tiers from production builders. The community's western Cedar Park location provides excellent access to Brushy Creek Trail, Lake Travis recreation, and the 183A corridor. Amenities are being built out in phases, with a community pool and parks already open and additional facilities planned. Walsh Ranch / Quarry is Cedar Park's answer to the master-planned community model that has driven Leander's growth.
lifestyle: Cedar Park's newest master-planned community with premium builders and Hill Country-adjacent character. Best for buyers who want new construction with Cedar Park's school and tax profile.

faqs.q: Is Walsh Ranch / Quarry in Cedar Park?
faqs.a: Yes. Walsh Ranch and the Quarry section are within Cedar Park city limits, with Leander ISD school assignments and Cedar Park's slightly lower base tax rate compared to Leander proper.

faqs.q: What is the tax rate in Walsh Ranch?
faqs.a: Walsh Ranch / Quarry's combined tax rate is approximately 2.38% in 2026, including a MUD district financing the new infrastructure. This is consistent with mid-range new Cedar Park development.

faqs.q: What schools serve Walsh Ranch?
faqs.a: Walsh Ranch is zoned to Leander ISD — CC Mason Elementary, Running Brushy Middle School, and Cedar Park High School.

faqs.q: Is Walsh Ranch still building?
faqs.a: Yes, Walsh Ranch / Quarry is actively developing as of 2026 with new phases from multiple premium builders including Toll Brothers, Taylor Morrison, and Coventry.

faqs.q: What makes Walsh Ranch unique?
faqs.a: The community's quarry heritage gives it a distinct design character with limestone-integrated landscaping that differentiates it from typical suburban developments. Premium builders and western Cedar Park location add to its appeal.

name: Santa Rita Ranch
overview: Santa Rita Ranch is Liberty Hill's premier master-planned community and one of the most decorated residential developments in all of Central Texas — the community has won multiple national and regional awards for its resort amenities and planning excellence. Built by a consortium of national and regional builders on 3,500+ acres, Santa Rita Ranch delivers an amenity package that rivals resort destinations: multiple pools including a lazy river, water slides, a full fitness complex, tennis courts, sports fields, and a lifestyle director who coordinates a packed annual events calendar. The community's Hill Country setting provides natural drama — rolling terrain, creek corridors, and live oak groves that form a backdrop for a genuinely aspirational lifestyle. Liberty Hill ISD schools serve the community, and the district has invested heavily in new facilities to serve the growth wave. The tradeoff is commute time: Austin is 45–55 minutes away, and the Domain closer to 40. For buyers who prioritize lifestyle over proximity, Santa Rita Ranch is the clear choice in the Hill Country northwest corridor.
commuteAustin: 45–55 min via US-183 or 183A to US-29
amenities: Lifestyle director and full events calendar
lifestyle: Resort living at suburban prices. Santa Rita Ranch attracts buyers who want their neighborhood to feel like a permanent vacation — facilities and programming that make staying home as appealing as going out.

faqs.q: Is Santa Rita Ranch worth the commute?
faqs.a: For buyers who prioritize lifestyle amenities and indoor/outdoor living over commute time, Santa Rita Ranch is consistently ranked as one of the best value propositions in the Austin metro. Many residents work remotely or adjust their schedule to avoid peak traffic.

faqs.q: What is the tax rate in Santa Rita Ranch?
faqs.a: Santa Rita Ranch's combined tax rate is approximately 2.42% in 2026, including a MUD district. Liberty Hill ISD's portion is slightly lower than LISD, partially offsetting the MUD addition.

faqs.q: What schools serve Santa Rita Ranch?
faqs.a: Santa Rita Ranch is zoned to Liberty Hill ISD — Santa Rita Elementary (on-site), Liberty Hill Middle School, and Liberty Hill High School. The district has invested in new campuses to serve the community's growth.

faqs.q: What awards has Santa Rita Ranch won?
faqs.a: Santa Rita Ranch has won multiple national Masterplan Community of the Year awards, recognizing its planning, amenities, and resident experience programming as among the best in the country.

faqs.q: What builders are in Santa Rita Ranch?
faqs.a: Santa Rita Ranch features a diverse builder lineup including Scott Felder, Chesmar, Perry Homes, Lennar, and First Texas Homes, offering product from the mid-$300s to $600K+.

name: Orchard Ridge
overview: Orchard Ridge is a newer master-planned community in Liberty Hill developed beginning around 2020, representing the wave of growth that followed Santa Rita Ranch's success in establishing Liberty Hill as a legitimate destination market. The community takes its name from the agricultural orchards that once occupied the Hill Country terrain where it now sits. Orchard Ridge's design emphasizes connection to the natural landscape — trails winding through cedar and live oak preserves, a community pond with surrounding greenspace, and homesite orientations that take advantage of the gentle hill country grade. Builders offer a mix of production and semi-custom product in the $310K–$480K range, making Orchard Ridge a more accessible entry point than some of Liberty Hill's premium communities. The commute to Austin runs 45–55 minutes, consistent with the broader Liberty Hill market. Liberty Hill ISD schools serve the community, with new campuses being built to accommodate the district's rapid enrollment growth.
lifestyle: Hill Country character in an accessible price range. Newer community feel with young homeowner demographics and an active HOA events calendar. Good option for first-time buyers wanting Liberty Hill's lifestyle at lower entry costs than Santa Rita Ranch.

faqs.q: How does Orchard Ridge compare to Santa Rita Ranch?
faqs.a: Orchard Ridge offers a lower entry price point ($310K vs $340K+) and a newer community feel, while Santa Rita Ranch has more established amenities and a larger events program. Both are excellent — the choice often comes down to budget and phase of community development.

faqs.q: What is the tax rate in Orchard Ridge?
faqs.a: Orchard Ridge's combined tax rate is approximately 2.38% in 2026, including a MUD district. Liberty Hill ISD's rate is slightly below Leander ISD, partially balancing the MUD addition.

faqs.q: What schools serve Orchard Ridge?
faqs.a: Orchard Ridge is zoned to Liberty Hill ISD — Rancho Sienna Elementary, Liberty Hill Middle School, and Liberty Hill High School. New campuses are being built to handle the district's rapid growth.

faqs.q: Is Orchard Ridge still developing?
faqs.a: Yes. Orchard Ridge is actively adding phases as of 2026, with new construction available from Highland Homes, Brightland, and Meritage across multiple price tiers.

faqs.q: What is Liberty Hill ISD like?
faqs.a: Liberty Hill ISD is one of the fastest-growing districts in Texas, with new campuses and increased funding from the community's development. Academic performance is improving alongside the investment, though it is not yet at the same historical depth as Leander ISD.

name: Whitestone
overview: Whitestone is a newer Liberty Hill community that takes its name from the limestone-rich geology of the Texas Hill Country — the white caliche and limestone outcroppings that define the landscape and the region's character. The community sits along FM 1869 in central Liberty Hill, conveniently positioned between the older Liberty Hill town square and the newer growth corridors opening along US-29 and US-183. Homes are primarily production-builder single-family product in the starter to mid-range price tier, making Whitestone one of Liberty Hill's most accessible entry points for first-time buyers and young families. Liberty Hill's small-town character — a quaint downtown square, local restaurants, and community events centered around the historic Briggs Building — provides a quality-of-life dimension not found in purely suburban developments. The community is still in active build-out with multiple phases planned.
amenities: Walking distance to Liberty Hill town square
lifestyle: Small-town Texas character with genuine downtown walkability to Liberty Hill's historic square. Best suited for buyers who value community character and affordability over commute efficiency.

faqs.q: Is Liberty Hill a good place to live?
faqs.a: Liberty Hill offers one of the most authentic small-town Texas experiences within commuting distance of Austin — a historic downtown, community events, and a genuine local identity that larger suburban cities lack. The trade-off is a longer commute to Austin's employment centers.

faqs.q: What is the tax rate in Whitestone Liberty Hill?
faqs.a: Whitestone's combined tax rate is approximately 2.33% in 2026, including a MUD district. Liberty Hill's overall tax structure is slightly more favorable than Leander's for comparable new construction.

faqs.q: What schools serve Whitestone?
faqs.a: Whitestone is zoned to Liberty Hill ISD — Bill Burden Elementary, Liberty Hill Middle School, and Liberty Hill High School.

faqs.q: Can I walk to downtown Liberty Hill from Whitestone?
faqs.a: Whitestone's central Liberty Hill location puts the historic downtown square within a short drive and some sections within walking distance — a genuine advantage over more remote Liberty Hill developments.

faqs.q: What builders are in Whitestone?
faqs.a: D.R. Horton, LGI Homes, and Pacesetter Homes are among the active builders in Whitestone, offering affordable new construction starting in the $290Ks.

name: Wolf Ranch
overview: Wolf Ranch is Georgetown's premier master-planned community, developed on a spectacular site along the South Fork of the San Gabriel River in south Georgetown. The community's centerpiece is its extraordinary river access — multi-acre river parks, swimming holes, and greenway trails along the San Gabriel create an amenity that money cannot simply build, making Wolf Ranch genuinely unique in the Austin metro. Developed beginning around 2016, Wolf Ranch attracted national builder attention immediately and features product from David Weekley, Scott Felder, Chesmar, and others. The community's proximity to Baylor Scott & White Medical Center Georgetown and the Dell Medical School's expanding presence in the area makes it particularly popular with healthcare and medical professionals. Georgetown's historic downtown square, nationally recognized for its Victorian-era commercial architecture, is minutes away and provides a quality dining, shopping, and cultural experience that suburban master-planned communities typically cannot access.
amenities: San Gabriel River access and river parks
Hike and bike trails along river
Proximity to Baylor Scott & White Georgetown
lifestyle: River community living with one of the Austin metro's most dramatic natural amenities. Popular with healthcare professionals, remote workers, and buyers seeking Georgetown's small-city quality of life.

faqs.q: What is Wolf Ranch's best feature?
faqs.a: Wolf Ranch's access to the San Gabriel River is unmatched in the Austin master-planned community market. Multi-acre river parks with swimming holes and trails create an amenity that distinguishes the community entirely from competitors.

faqs.q: What is the tax rate in Wolf Ranch Georgetown?
faqs.a: Wolf Ranch's combined tax rate is approximately 2.45% in 2026, including a MUD district. Georgetown ISD rates are comparable to Leander ISD, making the overall tax burden similar to mid-range Leander developments.

faqs.q: What schools serve Wolf Ranch?
faqs.a: Wolf Ranch is zoned to Georgetown ISD — Wolf Ranch Elementary (on-site campus), James Tippit Middle School, and East View High School. Georgetown ISD has invested significantly in new facilities to serve the growth.

faqs.q: How far is Wolf Ranch from Austin?
faqs.a: Wolf Ranch is approximately 40–55 minutes from downtown Austin via I-35, or 50–60 minutes via SH-130. The I-35 route is fastest but subject to congestion during peak hours.

faqs.q: Is Georgetown a good place to live?
faqs.a: Georgetown consistently ranks among Texas's fastest-growing cities and has been named one of the best places to retire and live in Texas by multiple publications. The historic downtown, quality healthcare, and community character make it increasingly popular with buyers of all ages.

name: Berry Creek
overview: Berry Creek is Georgetown's established golf course community, anchored by the Berry Creek Country Club and its 18-hole championship course designed by Jack Nicklaus Jr. The community developed in the 1990s through 2010s and now represents one of Georgetown's most mature and desirable residential enclaves. The country club model drives Berry Creek's identity: golf membership, social events, a swimming pool, tennis courts, and a full-service clubhouse create a lifestyle that retirees, executives, and golf enthusiasts consistently seek out. Unlike newer Georgetown developments, Berry Creek features mature tree canopy, larger lots, and established landscaping that genuinely cannot be replicated in any new construction community. Home styles range from traditional single-story ranch to larger estate product, with custom and semi-custom work evident throughout. Georgetown's rapid growth means Berry Creek now benefits from excellent retail access that didn't exist a decade ago, while retaining the character of a more intimate, established neighborhood.
amenities: 18-hole Nicklaus Design golf course (Berry Creek Country Club)
lifestyle: Established country club lifestyle in Georgetown's most prestigious golf community. Attracts retirees, golf enthusiasts, and buyers seeking a mature neighborhood with genuine character and social infrastructure.

faqs.q: Who designed the Berry Creek golf course?
faqs.a: The Berry Creek Country Club course was designed by Jack Nicklaus Jr. (Nicklaus Design), giving it the kind of layout and pedigree that supports a full country club membership model.

faqs.q: What is the tax rate in Berry Creek Georgetown?
faqs.a: Berry Creek's combined tax rate is approximately 2.28% in 2026 — one of Georgetown's lower rates, as the community predates MUD districts. The absence of MUD debt service is a meaningful long-term savings advantage.

faqs.q: What schools serve Berry Creek?
faqs.a: Berry Creek is zoned to Georgetown ISD — Jo Ann Ford Elementary, James Tippit Middle School, and Georgetown High School.

faqs.q: Do I have to join the country club to live in Berry Creek?
faqs.a: Country club membership at Berry Creek Country Club is optional for residents. HOA membership is required and covers community common areas, but club membership (golf, pool, fitness, dining) is a separate voluntary expense.

faqs.q: Is Berry Creek a good investment?
faqs.a: Berry Creek's mature trees, established lots, Nicklaus-designed golf course, and Georgetown ISD schools create a combination of lifestyle and investment characteristics that has supported consistent appreciation. The community is largely built out, meaning scarcity supports values.
